description: Disease name field stored as text. Contains 41 distinct, non-null values, all unique within the table, with no missing values. Acts as a one-to-one shared join key with symptom_precaution.Disease (97.6% bilateral coverage). Quality observations include one lowercase entry ('hepatitis A') and three apparent spelling deviations ('Peptic ulcer diseae', 'Osteoarthristis', '(vertigo) Paroymsal  Positional Vertigo').
recommended_actions: [STANDARDIZE] Correct deterministic spelling and casing deviations: 'hepatitis A' → 'Hepatitis A', 'Peptic ulcer diseae' → 'Peptic ulcer disease', 'Osteoarthristis' → 'Osteoarthritis', '(vertigo) Paroymsal  Positional Vertigo' → '(vertigo) Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo'. | [VALIDATE] Enforce referential integrity against symptom_precaution.Disease before joining or loading new records.

sample_values: Intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH) is when blood suddenly bursts into brain tissue, causing damage to your brain. Symptoms usually appear suddenly during ICH. They include headache, weakness, confusion, and paralysis, particularly on one side of your body. | Impetigo (im-puh-TIE-go) is a common and highly contagious skin infection that mainly affects infants and children. Impetigo usually appears as red sores on the face, especially around a child's nose and mouth, and on hands and feet. The sores burst and develop honey-colored crusts. | Osteoarthritis is the most common form of arthritis, affecting millions of people worldwide. It occurs when the protective cartilage that cushions the ends of your bones wears down over time. | An acute illness characterized by fever caused by infection with the bacterium Salmonella typhi. Typhoid fever has an insidious onset, with fever, headache, constipation, malaise, chills, and muscle pain. Diarrhea is uncommon, and vomiting is not usually severe. | Psoriasis is a common skin disorder that forms thick, red, bumpy patches covered with silvery scales. They can pop up anywhere, but most appear on the scalp, elbows, knees, and lower back. Psoriasis can't be passed from person to person. It does sometimes happen in members of the same family.
description: Free-text descriptions of medical conditions and diseases. All 41 records are non-null and unique, with expected structural variation typical of natural language medical summaries.
